title: Sleep
definition: A naturally recurring state of reduced consciousness and sensory activity essential for health, consisting of sleep cycles of non-REM and REM sleep.

difficulty_levels.beginner: A period when our bodies rest, heal, and process information, divided into different stages such as dreaming and deep sleep.
difficulty_levels.intermediate: The periodic state of reduced consciousness and sensory activity that is crucial for health, characterized by distinct sleep stages like REM (rapid eye movement) sleep and non-REM sleep.
difficulty_levels.expert: The recurring state of reduced consciousness and sensory activity essential for health, involving different stages such as REM sleep and deep non-REM sleep, which are crucial for physical restoration, memory consolidation, and cognitive functions. The circadian rhythm regulates these cycles.

extra_fields.insight: Rest is not merely a pause but an active period of healing and information processing
